claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to understand a bit about your child or children.

**First question: How old is your child?**

This is important because eligibility depends on age:
- Children **under 16** are generally eligible
- Children **aged 16-19** may be eligible if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** are not eligible for Child Benefit

Could you tell me:
1. How many children do you want to check eligibility for?
2. What is the age (or ages) of your child/children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I have one child - Alex is 9 years old and lives at home with me. I just want to check if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me work through Alex's eligibility with you.

Since Alex is 9 years old, he falls into the **under 16** category. Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is 9 years old, I'm assuming he's not in paid work, but I need to confirm this to proceed. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's not working at all - he's only 9, so he's still in primary school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That makes sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in Alex's own name (not yours as the parent). 

For a 9-year-old in primary school, this would be very unusual. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. It's just me as the parent who might claim things for us.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship program?**

Given that Alex is only 9 years old and in primary school, I'm assuming he's not in an apprenticeship (which typically applies to older teenagers), but I need to confirm this for the assessment. Can you confirm Alex is not in an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - he's far too young for that! He's just doing regular primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes complete sense! Let me move forward.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. This is an important requirement for Child Benefit.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You'll qualify if **ANY** of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common situation)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources to support your family
6. You have pre-settled status as a student with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Which of these applies to your situation?** Most people simply normally live in the UK - is that your case?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - I normally live in the UK. I live in Manchester with Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That satisfies the residency requirement. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to confirm your responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You mentioned that Alex lives at home with you in Manchester, which means you meet the responsibility requirement. To be clear, you qualify if **ANY** of these apply:

1. **You live with the child** (which you've already told me is the case)
2. You contribute to the child's upkeep (paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards their care)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

Since Alex lives with you, you clearly meet the first requirement. Can you confirm that Alex lives with you full-time?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex lives with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check something important about other potential claimants.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- A grandparent
- Another guardian or family member

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. 

What's the situation - are you the only person claiming (or planning to claim) for Alex, or is someone else already claiming for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me - I'm the only one who would be claiming for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me move forward with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now I need to check Alex's living situation to see if any special rules apply.

**Which of the following describes Alex's current situation?**

1. **Living normally at home with you** (most common)
2. Currently in hospital
3. In local authority care
4. Being fostered

You mentioned Alex lives at home with you in Manchester, so I'm assuming it's option 1 - living normally at home. Is that correct, or is Alex currently in hospital, care, or a foster arrangement?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is just living normally at home with me. He's not in hospital or care or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Almost there! Just one final question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council paying for Alex's living costs or care? Since Alex lives at home with you normally, I expect the answer is that the council is NOT paying for him, but I need to confirm this.

Can you confirm that your local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's costs. I'm supporting him myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Let me record the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
# 🎉 Great News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE** ✓

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for Alex.

## Why You're Eligible:

✓ Alex is 9 years old (under 16 - automatically qualifies)  
✓ Alex is not in paid work  
✓ Alex doesn't receive benefits in his own right  
✓ Alex is not in an apprenticeship  
✓ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)  
✓ You have responsibility for Alex (living together)  
✓ No one else is claiming for Alex  
✓ Alex lives normally at home with you  
✓ The council is not paying for Alex's costs  

## Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earn over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge (but you can still claim)
5. **Payments**: Made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

## How to Apply:

You can apply online at: **https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim**

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16 - automatic qualification age; (2) Alex is not in paid work (still in primary school); (3) Alex does not receive any benefits in his own right; (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) The claimant has responsibility for Alex as they live together full-time; (7) No other person is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex is living normally at home, not in hospital, care, or foster arrangement; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria have been met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 9-year-old child Alex who lives with them in Manchester. No disqualifying factors are present.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

All eligibility criteria have been met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements for their 9-year-old child Alex who lives with them in Manchester. No disqualifying factors are present.
